zh.lifeSummary: Richard Colet Of The Hale At Wendover是Henry Colet和Mrs Henry Colet的兒子，於1380年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福。他於1395年與Margaret Colet Of the Hale At Wendover在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1461年在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾文多福享年81歲。

nameMeanings.name: Colet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Philippe, Rodrigue.
French: from a shortened form of a personal name such as Renolet, a pet form of Renaud . Compare Nolette .

variants.name: Renaud
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Normand, Gilles, Adelard, Gisele, Cecile, Chantal, Emile, Gaston, Germaine.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ragin ‘counsel’ + wald ‘rule’, an equivalent to English Reynold . Compare Arno , Erno , Rayno , Reneau , Reno , and Reynaud .

variants.name: Nolette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alderic, Armand, Aurore, Lucienne, Normand, Philippe, Raoul.
Altered form of French Nolet , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Nollette .

variants.name: Nollette
meanings: Altered form of French   Nolet  . Compare   Nolette  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Colet Of The Hale At Wendover was born in 1380, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England as the son of Henry Colet and Mrs Henry Colet. He married Margaret Colet Of the Hale At Wendover in 1395, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1461, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 81.

Il a épousé Margaret Colet Of the Hale At Wendover en 1395, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1461, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 81 ans.
